description_long: This object consists of two identical benches placed side by side, each featuring a long, flat seat with a slatted backrest and three supporting legs. The benches are constructed with a sturdy frame, likely made of metal, and have a green seat and backrest surface, suggesting a painted or coated finish. The backrest is composed of evenly spaced vertical slats, providing both support and ventilation. The legs are straight and simple, with crossbars for added stability. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, suitable for high-traffic public spaces. The benches are likely intended for outdoor use, such as in parks or along sidewalks, where durability and weather resistance are important.
description: A pair of green, slatted-back benches with metal frames and three straight legs each, designed for public outdoor seating. The benches feature sturdy construction and a utilitarian style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the slatted backrests and full length of both benches side by side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the benches and the straight, simple leg design.
description_view_2: Another front view, nearly identical to view 0, showing the slatted backrests and seat surfaces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the depth and leg structure of the benches.
attribute_leg_design: three straight metal legs per bench with crossbars
